extraction.one_line_summary: MATTHEWS Michael won Stage 16 after Sunweb pre-split peloton on opening climb then Sky decimated field in crosswinds at km ~160 (20km to go)
tactical_patterns: Sunweb publicly telegraphed dual-phase plan (early climb splits, late crosswind positioning) yet still executed flawlessly against informed peloton.
Sky triggered decisive crosswind echelon at km ~160 (20km to go) on righthand wind section, more damaging than mountain attacks.
Quick-Step spread thin protecting Kittel's green jersey cost Martin team support; domestiques off-road chasing shows resource allocation failure.
Aru/Uran showed superior crosswind positioning awareness compared to Martin, following Sky rotations rather than own teammates before km 180 split.

rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: executed work on opening climb, intermediate sprint, and crosswind chase yet still won finale sprint—exceptional diesel engine capacity
rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: negative: lost 12 minutes and conceded 20 intermediate sprint points to Matthews; Quick-Step's green jersey defense collapsing under sunweb pressure
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: negative: caught out of position before km 180 crosswind split despite universal knowledge of danger zone; lost 50 seconds without teammates
rider_signals.BUHAN Nils: neutral: bridged 2-minute gap solo over 30km but gain negated by Sky's later crosswind attack; wasted diesel effort
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: positive: positioned on Sky wheels before crosswind split rather than Astana teammates, reading race better than GC rivals
rider_signals.FROOME Christopher: positive: Sky's crosswind execution more effective than mountain attacks for isolating rivals; team confidence visibly growing per Froome interview
